texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: PERMEABILITY OF  CHLOROMETHANES IN  COPOLYMERS OF  CHLOROPRENE WITH METHYL METHACRYLATE AND METHACRYLIC ACID*
texts.text: PERMEABILITY OF  CHLOROMETHANES IN  COPOLYMERS OF  CHLOROPRENE WITH METHYL METHACRYLATE AND METHACRYLIC ACID*

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The permeability of  chloromethanes CH,~CI4_m with  4  > m > 0,  C2H2 and  C2H 6  hydrocarbons, and  also H2, N2,  and  02 in  polybutadiene, polyvinyltrimethylsilane, d  chloroprene-methyl methacrylate-methacrylic acid terpolymers containing 13.8-15% of  methyl  methacrylate and  0-8% of  methacrylic acid is studied. The chloromethane  permeability coefficients of  polychloroprene and its copolymers  are  comparatively low compared  with  that of  polybutadiene, but  rather high  compared  with  polyvinyltrimethylsilane.
texts.text: The permeability of  chloromethanes CH,~CI4_m with  4  > m > 0,  C2H2 and  C2H 6  hydrocarbons, and  also H2, N2,  and  02 in  polybutadiene, polyvinyltrimethylsilane, d  chloroprene-methyl methacrylate-methacrylic acid terpolymers containing 13.8-15% of  methyl  methacrylate and  0-8% of  methacrylic acid is studied. The chloromethane  permeability coefficients of  polychloroprene and its copolymers  are  comparatively low compared  with  that of  polybutadiene, but  rather high  compared  with  polyvinyltrimethylsilane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: THE  SUCCESS  achieved in  the  membrane separation of  gases is mainly  due  to  the use  of  membranes based  on glass-like polymers. The use  of  such  polymers  as  polysulphone, polyvinyltrimethylsilane (PVTMS),  cellulose acetate, etc. for  the  extraction and concentration of  HE, O2, and CO2  from mixtures of  volatile gases  has  become possible because  these polymers  have  comparatively high permeability coefficients P for  these  gases, because  the  coefficients of  diffusion D are  markedly dependent  on the  size of  the  diffusant molecules, and because  they  can  be used  to  obtain thin, almost  perfect membranes.
texts.text: THE  SUCCESS  achieved in  the  membrane separation of  gases is mainly  due  to  the use  of  membranes based  on glass-like polymers. The use  of  such  polymers  as  polysulphone, polyvinyltrimethylsilane (PVTMS),  cellulose acetate, etc. for  the  extraction and concentration of  HE, O2, and CO2  from mixtures of  volatile gases  has  become possible because  these polymers  have  comparatively high permeability coefficients P for  these  gases, because  the  coefficients of  diffusion D are  markedly dependent  on the  size of  the  diffusant molecules, and because  they  can  be used  to  obtain thin, almost  perfect membranes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As research on the  effect of  the  MM of  diffusants on their permeability coefficients in  different polymers [1, 2]  shows,  with  different glass-like polymers  and rubbers there are  qualitative differences in  the  types of  relations obtained: with  glasses the  values of  P  are  inversely related to  the  MM, whereas  with  rubbers they  are  directly related to  it. This  determines the  strategy used  in  selecting the  membrane polymer  material. If  the  problem  is  the  concentration of  the  vapours  of  organic compounds from  their mixtures with  volatile gases  (air, methane,  etc.) then  the  permeate will  be enriched in  the  heavy  components, provided the  membrane is  obtained from  high  elastic material.
texts.text: As research on the  effect of  the  MM of  diffusants on their permeability coefficients in  different polymers [1, 2]  shows,  with  different glass-like polymers  and rubbers there are  qualitative differences in  the  types of  relations obtained: with  glasses the  values of  P  are  inversely related to  the  MM, whereas  with  rubbers they  are  directly related to  it. This  determines the  strategy used  in  selecting the  membrane polymer  material. If  the  problem  is  the  concentration of  the  vapours  of  organic compounds from  their mixtures with  volatile gases  (air, methane,  etc.) then  the  permeate will  be enriched in  the  heavy  components, provided the  membrane is  obtained from  high  elastic material.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: However, there  is  hardly  any published data  on the  permeability parameters of  rubbers  of different chemical  nature  and different physical properties in  relation to  the  vapours  of  organic compounds.
texts.text: However, there  is  hardly  any published data  on the  permeability parameters of  rubbers  of different chemical  nature  and different physical properties in  relation to  the  vapours  of  organic compounds.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In this  work the  features of the  transfer of  films  based on polychloroprene (PCP) and its copolymers with  MAA and  methacrylic acid (MAC)  are  studied. It is known that the  introduction of small  amounts  of  these monomers in  the  emulsion  copolymerization of  chloroprene (CP) greatly improves  the  mechanical and film-forming characteristics of  the  polymers [3]. A  series of  chloromethanes  from CH4  to CCl  4 was selected as  model diffusants. Movement  along  this series of diffusants provides a means of  regularly varying  the  size  of  the  molecules, the  polarity of  the diffusant, the  tendency to  form  hydrogen bonds,  and  other physical properties, and  accordingly of following the  effect of  these factors on  the  transfer properties. Furthermore, the  transfer parameters
texts.text: In this  work the  features of the  transfer of  films  based on polychloroprene (PCP) and its copolymers with  MAA and  methacrylic acid (MAC)  are  studied. It is known that the  introduction of small  amounts  of  these monomers in  the  emulsion  copolymerization of  chloroprene (CP) greatly improves  the  mechanical and film-forming characteristics of  the  polymers [3]. A  series of  chloromethanes  from CH4  to CCl  4 was selected as  model diffusants. Movement  along  this series of diffusants provides a means of  regularly varying  the  size  of  the  molecules, the  polarity of  the diffusant, the  tendency to  form  hydrogen bonds,  and  other physical properties, and  accordingly of following the  effect of  these factors on  the  transfer properties. Furthermore, the  transfer parameters

texts.label: text
texts.orig: of certain  permanently  present  gases (H2, N2,  02), and  hydrocarbons  (C2H2,  C2H6)  were determined.
texts.text: of certain  permanently  present  gases (H2, N2,  02), and  hydrocarbons  (C2H2,  C2H6)  were determined.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Emulsion  polymerization was used  to  obtain PCP and  its copolymers, containing 13.8-15% MMA and different quantities of  MAA (0--8%), using  the  method described in  Ref.  [3].  The reaction conditions used ensured  close  to 100%  conversion, so that  the  composition  of the  copolymer corresponded  to the quantity of monomers  introduced  into  the reaction. Films of thickness 30-150/~m  were  cast from  the  aqueous  latexes on to  glass. Before  the  measurements  the  films were subjected to  vacuum treatment at  60°C  over  a  week.  The permeability coefficients were  determined by mass spectrometry [4].  During the  measurements  the  vapour  pressure above the  membrane (liquid under normal conditions of  the  penetrants CCI4, CHCI3,  and CH2C12)  was varied  from 10  mmHg to  gas  saturation (within the  range  10-760  mmHg).  The pressure above  the  membrane was 10  -3  mmHg.
texts.text: Emulsion  polymerization was used  to  obtain PCP and  its copolymers, containing 13.8-15% MMA and different quantities of  MAA (0--8%), using  the  method described in  Ref.  [3].  The reaction conditions used ensured  close  to 100%  conversion, so that  the  composition  of the  copolymer corresponded  to the quantity of monomers  introduced  into  the reaction. Films of thickness 30-150/~m  were  cast from  the  aqueous  latexes on to  glass. Before  the  measurements  the  films were subjected to  vacuum treatment at  60°C  over  a  week.  The permeability coefficients were  determined by mass spectrometry [4].  During the  measurements  the  vapour  pressure above the  membrane (liquid under normal conditions of  the  penetrants CCI4, CHCI3,  and CH2C12)  was varied  from 10  mmHg to  gas  saturation (within the  range  10-760  mmHg).  The pressure above  the  membrane was 10  -3  mmHg.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Table 1 shows the  mechanical  properties of  the  polymers. As can be seen,  the  introduction of acrylic monomers  results in  a marked increase in  the  relative elongation at  rupture Al and the ultimate tensile stress S.  On  the  other  hand,  the  brittleness temperature and the  glass formation temperature are  changed  relatively little over  the  range  -40--30°C. Over the  range  -40-+  130°C, using the DSC method  for  studying  the copolymers  a single  glass  formation  temperature  is observed, which  indicates the  absence of  heterophase character in  the  copolymers studied. The data from  IR  spectroscopy and  X-ray  diffraction analysis show that there are  no  crystalline phases  in  any of  the  copolymers.
texts.text: Table 1 shows the  mechanical  properties of  the  polymers. As can be seen,  the  introduction of acrylic monomers  results in  a marked increase in  the  relative elongation at  rupture Al and the ultimate tensile stress S.  On  the  other  hand,  the  brittleness temperature and the  glass formation temperature are  changed  relatively little over  the  range  -40--30°C. Over the  range  -40-+  130°C, using the DSC method  for  studying  the copolymers  a single  glass  formation  temperature  is observed, which  indicates the  absence of  heterophase character in  the  copolymers studied. The data from  IR  spectroscopy and  X-ray  diffraction analysis show that there are  no  crystalline phases  in  any of  the  copolymers.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Since  no  MM regulators were  used  in  polymerization, the  copolymers had  a  cross-linked structure and were  almost  insoluble in  organic solvents.
texts.text: Since  no  MM regulators were  used  in  polymerization, the  copolymers had  a  cross-linked structure and were  almost  insoluble in  organic solvents.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A  study  of  the  latex particle size by scattering of  laser light (Nanosaizer, England)  showed that with  all the  copolymers  studied, and also PCP a  unimodal  particle size distribution with  particles of maximum  size 163/xm was characteristic.
texts.text: A  study  of  the  latex particle size by scattering of  laser light (Nanosaizer, England)  showed that with  all the  copolymers  studied, and also PCP a  unimodal  particle size distribution with  particles of maximum  size 163/xm was characteristic.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In  the  CHnCI4_ n  chloromethane series studied, in  which  4  > n  > 0,  the  physical properties of  the diffusants are  varied over  wide limits: molecule  dimensions  from 3.758  to  5.947/~, boiling point from -161.6  to  76.8°C,  parameter of  Lennard-Jones potential from 148.6  to  507.6  K. Although  the extreme  terms  of  this series are  non-polar, the  dipole moments of  CH3C1,  CH2CI2, and CHCI3  are approximately 1.86,  1.57,  and 1.15  Debye. Hydrogen bond formation  is  characteristic of  these compounds,  especially CHCI3. Measurement of  P and D  for  this series enables the  effect of  these factors on the  diffusional and thermodynamic  properties of  the  polymers to  be  followed.
texts.text: In  the  CHnCI4_ n  chloromethane series studied, in  which  4  > n  > 0,  the  physical properties of  the diffusants are  varied over  wide limits: molecule  dimensions  from 3.758  to  5.947/~, boiling point from -161.6  to  76.8°C,  parameter of  Lennard-Jones potential from 148.6  to  507.6  K. Although  the extreme  terms  of  this series are  non-polar, the  dipole moments of  CH3C1,  CH2CI2, and CHCI3  are approximately 1.86,  1.57,  and 1.15  Debye. Hydrogen bond formation  is  characteristic of  these compounds,  especially CHCI3. Measurement of  P and D  for  this series enables the  effect of  these factors on the  diffusional and thermodynamic  properties of  the  polymers to  be  followed.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: data  in  Fig. 1  and  Table 2,  the  values of  P are  in  most  cases  much more dependent on  p than  the coefficients D. This  can  indicate that the  dependence  of p on  the  pressure is  determined mainly  by the  lack of  linearity of  the  sorption isotherm, and  not  by  the  plasticizing effect of  the  vapours. In fact, the  effective solubility factors o'en = P/D sharply increase with  increase in  p,  i.e. the  isotherm should  be  convex  relative to  the  horizontal axis. This  type  of  isotherm is  usual for  the  sorption of vapours  in polymers  above  the  glass transition temperature [5].
texts.text: data  in  Fig. 1  and  Table 2,  the  values of  P are  in  most  cases  much more dependent on  p than  the coefficients D. This  can  indicate that the  dependence  of p on  the  pressure is  determined mainly  by the  lack of  linearity of  the  sorption isotherm, and  not  by  the  plasticizing effect of  the  vapours. In fact, the  effective solubility factors o'en = P/D sharply increase with  increase in  p,  i.e. the  isotherm should  be  convex  relative to  the  horizontal axis. This  type  of  isotherm is  usual for  the  sorption of vapours  in polymers  above  the  glass transition temperature [5].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When  p  < 10 mmHg P and D  are  independent of  concentration. Under these  conditions it is convenient to  follow the  effect of  the  nature of  the  penetrant and  the polymer  on  the  values of  P and D and  the  solubility coefficient, which  can  be  calculated from  the  equation tr = P/D.
texts.text: When  p  < 10 mmHg P and D  are  independent of  concentration. Under these  conditions it is convenient to  follow the  effect of  the  nature of  the  penetrant and  the polymer  on  the  values of  P and D and  the  solubility coefficient, which  can  be  calculated from  the  equation tr = P/D.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For PCP, in  moving along  the CH4-CCI4 series the  values of  P increase, and pass  through  a maximum  for CHCI 3. Since  in  this series the  size of  the  diffusant increases, it must  be  assumed  that thermodynamic factors have  a  specific effect. The nature of  the  relation is retained for copolymers, but  the  P maximum  is  displaced to  CH2C12. This  indicates the  considerable role of  the  diffusional permeability component,  which  is due  to  an  increase in  chain stiffness and  an  increase in  inter-chain interactions on introducing acrylic monomers.  The  monomer units of  the  MAA have  a  much greater effect on the transfer properties than the  MMA units: introduction of 15%  MMA into  the copolymer  composition results in  approximately the  same  changes in  the  permeability coefficient as introduction of  8%  MAA.
texts.text: For PCP, in  moving along  the CH4-CCI4 series the  values of  P increase, and pass  through  a maximum  for CHCI 3. Since  in  this series the  size of  the  diffusant increases, it must  be  assumed  that thermodynamic factors have  a  specific effect. The nature of  the  relation is retained for copolymers, but  the  P maximum  is  displaced to  CH2C12. This  indicates the  considerable role of  the  diffusional permeability component,  which  is due  to  an  increase in  chain stiffness and  an  increase in  inter-chain interactions on introducing acrylic monomers.  The  monomer units of  the  MAA have  a  much greater effect on the transfer properties than the  MMA units: introduction of 15%  MMA into  the copolymer  composition results in  approximately the  same  changes in  the  permeability coefficient as introduction of  8%  MAA.

The sharpest increase is  observed  with  diffusants of  high  MM and bulk (CHC13 and  CC14).  On the  other hand,  the  effective solubility coefficients are  comparatively little

texts.label: text
texts.orig: dependent  on the  copolymer  composition. The decrease in  the  diffusion coefficients of  gases  on introducing acrylate monomer  units into PCP could  be  associated with  the  action of  two  factors: on the  one  hand,  as  a  result of  the  copolymerization of  CP and  MMA the  stiffness of  the  macrochains increases, which  is  reflected in  an  increase in  the  glass-transition temperature (Table  1), and  on the other  hand,  in  the  case  of  ternary copolymers  containing carboxyl groups  in  the  MAA monomer units, additional physical cross-linking is  possible, due  to  the  formation of  hydrogen  bonds  [6].
texts.text: dependent  on the  copolymer  composition. The decrease in  the  diffusion coefficients of  gases  on introducing acrylate monomer  units into PCP could  be  associated with  the  action of  two  factors: on the  one  hand,  as  a  result of  the  copolymerization of  CP and  MMA the  stiffness of  the  macrochains increases, which  is  reflected in  an  increase in  the  glass-transition temperature (Table  1), and  on the other  hand,  in  the  case  of  ternary copolymers  containing carboxyl groups  in  the  MAA monomer units, additional physical cross-linking is  possible, due  to  the  formation of  hydrogen  bonds  [6].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In  analysing the  transfer and thermodynamic  properties of  polymers, a correlation is  generally constructed between  the  solubility coefficients orand the  Lennard-Jones potential parameter e/k, the  critical temperature Tcr, or  the  normal  boiling point  Tboi [5]. Fulfilment of  these  correlations indicates the Van  der Waals character of the polymer-sorbate interaction. In a number  of chloromethanes between  CH4  and CCI4 these  values  are  changed steadily and lie within  a wide range,  which opens up the possibility of explaining the nature  of their  interaction with the membrane material. Figure 2  shows  plots of  log  or against Tboi. The straight lines are  obtained by  the least squares  method for  non-polar molecules  (CH4, C2H2,  C2H6,  CC14). The linear correlation parameters for polymers  of  different composition are  close. As can  be  seen, the  values of  or for polar sorbates and sorbates capable  of  forming  hydrogen  bonds  (CHaCI,  CH2CI2, CHCI3) in  all cases lie somewhat higher  than  the  calculated straight lines (the values of  or for  the  different polymers  differ by  a  factor of  1.5-3). Approximately identical deviations are  observed as  between  the  experimental and calculated values for  polymers  containing MMA and MAA,  and  for  PCP, and also for  sorbates with  different dipole moments and a  tendency to  hydrogen  bond formation. The observed  increase in  the  solubility coefficients is  obviously associated with  dipole--dipole interaction, and with  the formation of  hydrogen  bonds.  Less  ambiguous  data  on the  role of  these factors can  be  obtained by studying the  thermodynamics  of  sorption by  these copolymers  by  reversed gas  chromatography.
texts.text: In  analysing the  transfer and thermodynamic  properties of  polymers, a correlation is  generally constructed between  the  solubility coefficients orand the  Lennard-Jones potential parameter e/k, the  critical temperature Tcr, or  the  normal  boiling point  Tboi [5]. Fulfilment of  these  correlations indicates the Van  der Waals character of the polymer-sorbate interaction. In a number  of chloromethanes between  CH4  and CCI4 these  values  are  changed steadily and lie within  a wide range,  which opens up the possibility of explaining the nature  of their  interaction with the membrane material. Figure 2  shows  plots of  log  or against Tboi. The straight lines are  obtained by  the least squares  method for  non-polar molecules  (CH4, C2H2,  C2H6,  CC14). The linear correlation parameters for polymers  of  different composition are  close. As can  be  seen, the  values of  or for polar sorbates and sorbates capable  of  forming  hydrogen  bonds  (CHaCI,  CH2CI2, CHCI3) in  all cases lie somewhat higher  than  the  calculated straight lines (the values of  or for  the  different polymers  differ by  a  factor of  1.5-3). Approximately identical deviations are  observed as  between  the  experimental and calculated values for  polymers  containing MMA and MAA,  and  for  PCP, and also for  sorbates with  different dipole moments and a  tendency to  hydrogen  bond formation. The observed  increase in  the  solubility coefficients is  obviously associated with  dipole--dipole interaction, and with  the formation of  hydrogen  bonds.  Less  ambiguous  data  on the  role of  these factors can  be  obtained by studying the  thermodynamics  of  sorption by  these copolymers  by  reversed gas  chromatography.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: independent of  pressure, the  noted  marked  effect of  pressure on  the  permeability of  the  vapours of liquid chloromethane (CH2CI2, CHCI3, CC14) is the  factor determining the  increase in  selectivity of "organic vapour-permanently present gas"  mixtures. Figure 3  shows  plots of  the  separation factors a~N:  = Pi/PN2 (where  Pi  represents the  permeability factors of  the  chloromethanes) against p,  where Pi  and  PN,  are measured  for the  individual components. In  passing from  infinitely diluted vapours to saturation, i.e. over  the  activity range O<p/ps< 1,  the  values of  Pi  and ai/N2 increase sharply. A larger increase is noted  for chloroform, so  that the  order of  change of  Pi  and ai/N2 for the  diluted and saturated vapours  in  the  CCI4-CHCIa-CH2C12 series differs. The factors for  the  separation of saturated vapours  of  chloromethanes and nitrogen are  the  higher the  greater the  tendency of  the given  diffusant to  form  hydrogen  bonds  (maximum for chloroform).
texts.text: independent of  pressure, the  noted  marked  effect of  pressure on  the  permeability of  the  vapours of liquid chloromethane (CH2CI2, CHCI3, CC14) is the  factor determining the  increase in  selectivity of "organic vapour-permanently present gas"  mixtures. Figure 3  shows  plots of  the  separation factors a~N:  = Pi/PN2 (where  Pi  represents the  permeability factors of  the  chloromethanes) against p,  where Pi  and  PN,  are measured  for the  individual components. In  passing from  infinitely diluted vapours to saturation, i.e. over  the  activity range O<p/ps< 1,  the  values of  Pi  and ai/N2 increase sharply. A larger increase is noted  for chloroform, so  that the  order of  change of  Pi  and ai/N2 for the  diluted and saturated vapours  in  the  CCI4-CHCIa-CH2C12 series differs. The factors for  the  separation of saturated vapours  of  chloromethanes and nitrogen are  the  higher the  greater the  tendency of  the given  diffusant to  form  hydrogen  bonds  (maximum for chloroform).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The separation factors for  the  active vapours  and the  permanently present gases, as  calculated from  the  permeability of  the  individual components,  are  generally the  upper  limit of  selectivity of membrane separation by  means of  the  given  polymer. In  mass  transfer through membranes of  twoand multi-component  mixtures  the observed  separation factors are generally decreased. In connection  with this  the effect of the saturated vapours of liquid  chloromethanes on  the permeability coefficients ofthe  permanently present gases was  studied. The data for the  permeability  of oxygen in the  presence  of saturated vapours  show  an insignificant increase. This is  a consequence of  the  fact that, as  mentioned  above,  the  dependence of  the  permeability coefficients of  chloromethanes on  temperature is  determined not  by  plastification of  the  polymer, accompanied by a change  in  mobility of  the  macrochains and the  diffusion coefficients of the  gases, but  by the nonlinearity of  the  sorption isotherms. As a  result, the  separation factors for chloroform in  mixtures with  oxygen  and  air retain fairly high  values (Table 4).  A  similar conclusion as  to  the  retention of high  separation factors for  vapours of  organic compounds and  nitrogen was arrived at  by  Baker et al. [7] in  studying other high  elastic polymers.
texts.text: The separation factors for  the  active vapours  and the  permanently present gases, as  calculated from  the  permeability of  the  individual components,  are  generally the  upper  limit of  selectivity of membrane separation by  means of  the  given  polymer. In  mass  transfer through membranes of  twoand multi-component  mixtures  the observed  separation factors are generally decreased. In connection  with this  the effect of the saturated vapours of liquid  chloromethanes on  the permeability coefficients ofthe  permanently present gases was  studied. The data for the  permeability  of oxygen in the  presence  of saturated vapours  show  an insignificant increase. This is  a consequence of  the  fact that, as  mentioned  above,  the  dependence of  the  permeability coefficients of  chloromethanes on  temperature is  determined not  by  plastification of  the  polymer, accompanied by a change  in  mobility of  the  macrochains and the  diffusion coefficients of the  gases, but  by the nonlinearity of  the  sorption isotherms. As a  result, the  separation factors for chloroform in  mixtures with  oxygen  and  air retain fairly high  values (Table 4).  A  similar conclusion as  to  the  retention of high  separation factors for  vapours of  organic compounds and  nitrogen was arrived at  by  Baker et al. [7] in  studying other high  elastic polymers.

texts.text: TABLE 4. EFFECT OF SATURATED CHCI3  VAPOURS ON  THE  PERMEABILITY FACTORS OF 0 2 AND  THE SELECTIVITY ct(CHCI3/O2) AND  a(CHCIJAm), 25°C (COPOLYMER CP-MMA-MAA, [MAA]  = 1.5%)

texts.text: *Expressed in  cm 3  cm/cm 2  s  cm Hg.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It  is of  interest to  compare the  transfer properties of  the  chloroprene  copolymers  studied in  this work and other  polymers.  For this comparison  polybutadiene  (PB) was selected as  the  rubber,  and the  material of  the  gas  membrane  PVTMS was selected as  the  glass-like polymer.  The data  from the comparison are shown  in Table  5, where  the permeability  coefficients of three polymers are compared  (in  the  case  of  PVTMS they  are  evaluated  from the  permeability Q  of  an asymmetric membrane based on it), and also  the  permeabilities of  a membrane having a  working layer  of  the same thickness 0.25  p.m in  relation to  different chloromethanes. In  the  case of  the  rubbers  the  values of  Q  are  evaluated from the  permeability coefficients measured in  films of  thickness 30-60/~m.
texts.text: It  is of  interest to  compare the  transfer properties of  the  chloroprene  copolymers  studied in  this work and other  polymers.  For this comparison  polybutadiene  (PB) was selected as  the  rubber,  and the  material of  the  gas  membrane  PVTMS was selected as  the  glass-like polymer.  The data  from the comparison are shown  in Table  5, where  the permeability  coefficients of three polymers are compared  (in  the  case  of  PVTMS they  are  evaluated  from the  permeability Q  of  an asymmetric membrane based on it), and also  the  permeabilities of  a membrane having a  working layer  of  the same thickness 0.25  p.m in  relation to  different chloromethanes. In  the  case of  the  rubbers  the  values of  Q  are  evaluated from the  permeability coefficients measured in  films of  thickness 30-60/~m.

texts.label: caption

texts.text: TABLE 5. PERMEABILITY OF  CP-MMA-MAA COPOLYMER WITH [MAA]  = 1.5%, POLYBUTADIENE, AND POLYV1NYLTRIMETHYLSILANE

texts.text: rEvaluation  of  the  permeability  for  membranes of  effective thickness 0.25/.Lm (according  to  the  values  of P~  ).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The results of  Table  5  show that  the  CP  copolymers  are  rubbers  of  low  permeability. The values of  P for  these  are  less by one to  two orders  of  magnitude than  for  PB. The observed  differences in the  permeability coefficients of  PB, on the  one hand,  and PCP  and its copolymers  on the  other, are associated  with both the diffusional and  the thermodynamic  properties of the given rubbers. Comparison of  the  data  of  Table  3  with  the  values of  D  and ~r  for  PB  given  below  indicate this.
texts.text: The results of  Table  5  show that  the  CP  copolymers  are  rubbers  of  low  permeability. The values of  P for  these  are  less by one to  two orders  of  magnitude than  for  PB. The observed  differences in the  permeability coefficients of  PB, on the  one hand,  and PCP  and its copolymers  on the  other, are associated  with both the diffusional and  the thermodynamic  properties of the given rubbers. Comparison of  the  data  of  Table  3  with  the  values of  D  and ~r  for  PB  given  below  indicate this.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: On the  other hand,  the  values of  the  permeability coefficients for glass-like PVTMS in  relation to the  vapours of  organic substances are  still one  to two  orders of  magnitude lower  than for PCP and  its copolymers. This  reflects the  previously noted  trend [2] to  a  decrease in  the  permeability of  polymer glasses with  increase in  molecular weight of  the  diffusant, which  is associated with  predominance of the  role of  the  diffusional factor.
texts.text: On the  other hand,  the  values of  the  permeability coefficients for glass-like PVTMS in  relation to the  vapours of  organic substances are  still one  to two  orders of  magnitude lower  than for PCP and  its copolymers. This  reflects the  previously noted  trend [2] to  a  decrease in  the  permeability of  polymer glasses with  increase in  molecular weight of  the  diffusant, which  is associated with  predominance of the  role of  the  diffusional factor.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If  it is  assumed that defect-free membranes with  working  layers of  the  order of  1/zm can  be obtained from the  rubbers  studied, and that the  permeability coefficients for  such  thin  polymer layers and for  thick films  are  identical, then,  as  can  be seen  by comparing  the  values of  Q  for PVTMS and for  rubbers, the  membranes based on them should  have a high  output. Higher separation factors aura2 compared  with  membranes based  on  a  glass-like polymer  are  characteristic of  these.
texts.text: If  it is  assumed that defect-free membranes with  working  layers of  the  order of  1/zm can  be obtained from the  rubbers  studied, and that the  permeability coefficients for  such  thin  polymer layers and for  thick films  are  identical, then,  as  can  be seen  by comparing  the  values of  Q  for PVTMS and for  rubbers, the  membranes based on them should  have a high  output. Higher separation factors aura2 compared  with  membranes based  on  a  glass-like polymer  are  characteristic of  these.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Accordingly, in searching for  a membrane  material for  separating the vapours  of organic compounds from  their mixtures with  air and  other permanently present gases preference should be given to  high  elastic polymers. The selection of  a  specific polymer  is determined by  the  possibility of obtaining a composite membrane from  it with  sufficiently thin working  layers, its stability incontact with  the  mixture undergoing separation, and  the  constancy of  the  properties with  time.
texts.text: Accordingly, in searching for  a membrane  material for  separating the vapours  of organic compounds from  their mixtures with  air and  other permanently present gases preference should be given to  high  elastic polymers. The selection of  a  specific polymer  is determined by  the  possibility of obtaining a composite membrane from  it with  sufficiently thin working  layers, its stability incontact with  the  mixture undergoing separation, and  the  constancy of  the  properties with  time.
